claim: AI-generated fake photos of lost dogs at vet offices are being used by scammers to extort payment from desperate pet owners — a documented risk in the lost pet industry requiring owner verification protocols.
stance_target: sighting verification protocol — fake sighting reports as an operational risk in lost pet recovery
scope_conditions: Lost dog cases in the United States; documented case May 2026
quoted_support: Someone used AI to generate a fake photo of Posty lying on a table at a vet's office. Desperate and hopeful, Kurt paid their asking price and rushed there… only to find out Posty had never been there at all.

claim: Scent trail setup for food stations uses liquid smoke, BBQ sauce, and hot dogs — confirmed as standard MAR-network practice across multiple independent recovery teams.
stance_target: scent station protocol for lost dog food lure — specific compound combination
scope_conditions: Used by local volunteer trapper (Carole) in Pennsylvania case, May 2026
quoted_support: Local volunteer and trapper Carole joined the effort, setting trail cameras and creating an irresistible scent trail of liquid smoke, BBQ sauce, and hot dogs.

claim: Final recovery of a lost dog was achieved via a neighbor tip rather than thermal drone or trail camera — human social network remains the primary recovery mechanism even in high-tech searches.
stance_target: human social network vs. technology as primary recovery mechanism
scope_conditions: Single case; Golden Retriever, multi-state escape; Amish community and neighbor network involved
quoted_support: Flyers went up everywhere, the Amish community was alerted, neighbors spread the word, and an incredible network of people came together to help bring Posty home.
